name: Autophagy Induction surveillance coverage assessment for June 11, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Ordinary surveillance from the May 31, 2026 public baseline publication through the June 11, 2026 current-source search.

summary: The public Autophagy Induction claim is anchored by a coherent set of human spermidine and fasting-mimicking-diet records, now including a new older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot. Coverage remains thin because the pass surfaced older active/no-results registries, recent review context, and taxonomy-boundary questions that have not yet been reconciled into the public evidence map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline and staged update cover randomized human records for spermidine and fasting-mimicking-diet interventions, including positive short-term marker studies, a null longer-term cognition trial, safety/tolerability trials, and the new vaccine-response pilot.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage is enough for the current tentative claim, but active and no-results registries remain incomplete.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public claim is intervention-focused. Human observational dietary/polyamine or fasting-pattern evidence was not deeply audited in this surveillance pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A coverage-repair pass should decide whether observational dietary spermidine or fasting evidence is needed for boundary context.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current public outlook deliberately leans on human studies. Preclinical autophagy and spermidine anchors are present only indirectly through the cited human papers and reviews.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Seminal animal lifespan and immune-autophagy anchors should be audited if the track needs stronger mechanistic completeness.

evidence_categories.rationale: FMD trials and the staged spermidine vaccine pilot include autophagic flux, TFEB/autophagy-gene, immune-senescence, and metabolic-marker readouts.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The causal attribution to macroautophagy versus broader nutrient-sensing, mTOR, and immune-modulation pathways remains unresolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The SmartAge spermidine trial gives a duration-relevant null primary endpoint, while the high-purity spermidine trial limits claims about circulating polyamine movement and the staged vaccine pilot is explicitly small and subgroup-limited.
Small human studies suggest tolerability across spermidine formulations, but long-term safety, optimized dosing, and durability remain underbuilt.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The new vaccine pilot followed immune markers to 37 weeks, but it was not a long-term safety or clinical-outcome trial.

evidence_categories.rationale: The pass rechecked existing NCTs and identified additional older active, recruiting, completed, or unknown-status no-results registries around spermidine, fasting-mimicking diets, KH-1, and trehalose.
evidence_categories.gap_note: NCT07383311, NCT06186102, NCT07255300, NCT07354620, NCT06445569, NCT04928963, and NCT05593549 need coverage-repair triage.

evidence_categories.rationale: The PubMed delta search found June 2026 review-level context on polyamines/autophagy and vaccine immunosenescence, but this surveillance pass did not stage reviews because a primary human result was the material change.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review-level records such as PMID 42169620, PMID 42086115, and PMID 42225652 should be triaged during coverage repair.

evidence_categories.rationale: This pass did not attempt a seminal-anchor audit for autophagy induction biology.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should decide which canonical autophagy, spermidine, fasting, and immune-autophagy anchors belong in the public source map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The track overlaps immune rejuvenation, nutrient sensing, mTOR modulation, fasting/diet interventions, and disease-specific metabolic contexts.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should keep vaccine-response and immune-senescence signals from being overread as general autophagy or LEV progress.

search_log_summary: Ran scoped trial audit, ClinicalTrials.gov checks for all known NCT records, broad ClinicalTrials.gov searches for autophagy/spermidine/FMD/aging terms, PubMed ESearch/ESummary for May 31 to June 11 records, PubMed EFetch, and PMC BioC extraction for the material new spermidine pilot.
source_selection_notes: Staged PMID 42169618 because it is a new result-bearing randomized older-adult pilot directly testing spermidine, immune-cell autophagy biology, and vaccine-response endpoints.
Did not stage older active/no-results registry records because they predated the delta window or lacked results; they should be reconciled in coverage repair.
Did not stage review-level PubMed hits because the primary result was the material public update and review synthesis belongs in coverage repair.

known_gaps.description: Several active, recruiting, completed, or unknown-status no-results registries around spermidine, fasting-mimicking diet, KH-1, trehalose, and mixed diet/rapamycin interventions are not yet normalized or explicitly excluded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run coverage repair to stage or exclude the most relevant registries, starting with NCT07383311, NCT06186102, NCT07255300, NCT06445569, NCT04928963, and NCT05593549.

known_gaps.description: Recent reviews on polyamines/autophagy, vaccine immunosenescence, and broad aging intervention strategies were found but not synthesized into the source map.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Triage PMID 42169620, PMID 42086115, and PMID 42225652 during coverage repair.

known_gaps.description: Autophagy induction overlaps nutrient-sensing, immune rejuvenation, mTOR modulation, fasting, and spermidine/polyamine biology, which can make public attribution ambiguous.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During coverage repair, explicitly mark which sources support Autophagy Induction, which belong mainly to adjacent tracks, and which should remain excluded boundary context.

known_gaps.description: The field still lacks replicated human evidence that autophagy-inducing interventions produce long-lasting aging-relevant functional, disease, or mortality benefit.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public outlook tentative until larger trials tie autophagy measures to durable human outcomes.

next_coverage_action: Run coverage repair for active/no-results registry normalization, review triangulation, and taxonomy-boundary cleanup before the next ordinary surveillance pass.
